extraction.one_line_summary: DEGENT Thomas won from a 4-rider breakaway sprint after Fonda Pohl/Gilmay chase group failed to bridge, finishing 10 seconds behind.
tactical_patterns: Trek-Segafredo forced to chase despite Guillemata only 4:06 down GC, revealing threshold where breakaway GC threats trigger team control.
Fonda Pohl attacked with 46km remaining on climb, demonstrating elite rouleurs now initiate decisive selections much earlier than traditional final-climb timing.
Kemna attacked with 7.5km remaining while 38 seconds down GC, showing mid-distance attacks on technical finales to steal jerseys becoming standard.
Lotto-Soudal placed three riders in 21-man break, but over-representation failed when Fonda Pohl/others counter-attacked, suggesting numerical advantage neutralized by quality.

rider_signals.FONDA POHL Matteo: positive: launched winning move at 46km on climb, reaching final with enough to contest sprint, confirms climbing rouleur profile for long breakaway selections
rider_signals.DEGENT Thomas: positive: bookended decade of Grand Tour wins with tactical patience, winning 4-up sprint after Vanhoucke paced final 3-4km
rider_signals.GUILLEMATA Guillermo: neutral: only 4:06 down triggered full team chase from Trek, establishing precise GC gap threshold where breakaway freedom ends
rider_signals.LOPEZ Juan Pedro: positive: personally marked Kemna's late jersey-stealing attack despite leading race, unusual direct defending from maglia rosa
rider_signals.GILMAY Biniam: neutral: favorite took intermediate sprint points but chase with Fonda Pohl fell 10 seconds short, questioning sprint finish timing judgment
